sections.paragraph:
ἀποϑνῄσϰω [ἀπό, θνῄσκω] die

—a. of physical death Mt 8:32; Mk 5:35; Lk 16:22; J 6:49; Ro 5:8; Jd 12; Rv 8:11; plants J 12:24; 1 Cor 15:36; as prospect face death 1 Cor 15:31; 2 Cor 6:9; sim. ἀποϑνῄσϰοντες ἄνϑρωποι mortal people Hb 7:8. With focus on violent aspect, in effect functioning as pass. of ἀποϰτείνω = be killed Mt 26:35; J 12:33; Hb 10:28.

—b. in extended sense: negatively, as loss of life at a higher level than mere physical existence = spiritual death J 6:50 and oft.; Ro 7:10; 8:13; Rv 3:2; positively, as participatory experience in a new existence ἀπεϑάνομεν σὺν Χριστῷ we have died with Christ Ro 6:8; cp. Col 2:20.
